channel.title: math.LO updates on arXiv.org
channel.description: math.LO updates on the arXiv.org e-print archive.

item.title: An unusual example of a universal automorphism group
item.description: arXiv:2604.20501v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Let $M$ be a Fra\"{i}ss\'{e} structure (a countably infinite ultrahomogeneous structure). We refer to the class of structures embeddable in $M$ as the $\omega$-age of $M$. We consider the following two properties of $M$: we say that $M$ has a universal automorphism group if, for each $A$ in the $\omega$-age of $M$, there is an embedding $\textrm{Aut}(A) \to \textrm{Aut}(M)$, and we say that $M$ has group-extensible $\omega$-age if, for each $A$ in the $\omega$-age of $M$, there is an embedding $A \to M$ such that each automorphism of the image extends to an automorphism of $M$ and the extension map preserves group composition. It is immediate that if $M$ has group-extensible $\omega$-age, then $M$ has a universal automorphism group. We give an example of a Fra\"{i}ss\'{e} structure with a universal automorphism group whose $\omega$-age is not group-extensible, showing that the above two properties are not equivalent.
item.dc:creator: Rob Sullivan, Jeroen Winkel

item.title: On generic and supertight automorphisms
item.description: arXiv:2604.20415v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: We show that generic automorphisms of stable groups are supertight in a strong sense. In particular, we obtain the existence of supertight automorphisms. We also answer a question concerning the relationship between supertight automorphisms of $\mathrm{PGL}_2(K)$ and generic automorphisms of the underlying field $K$. Moreover, we provide partial evidence-already suggested by Hrushovski-toward the principle that ``fixed points are pseudofinite'' in the setting of generic automorphisms of simple groups of finite Morley rank.
item.dc:creator: Piotr Kowalski, P{\i}nar U\u{g}urlu Kowalski

item.title: Topological Dualities for Modal Algebras
item.description: arXiv:2604.20603v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: We display a family of Stone-type dualities linking categories of frames carrying pairs of modal operators to categories of spaces carrying a binary relation. Different notions of morphism used on the relational side lead to significant variations in the point construction. We show how the situation simplifies in the case of semicontinuous relations, allowing for straightforward correspondences between modal axioms and relational properties.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Collinson
